body: PT Transcoal Pacific Tbk (TCPI) has allocated IDR 700 billion for capital expenditure to expand its fleet of vessels by 2025, aiming to reach revenue of IDR 2.2 trillion by the end of 2025, with projected coal and nickel ore transportation volumes of 43.43 million metric tons and 31,500 kiloliters of crude palm oil. Despite a 9% year-on-year revenue increase in September 2024, TCPI has revised its 2024 revenue projection downward to IDR 1.95 trillion due to adverse weather conditions.
title: PT Transcoal Pacific sets aside IDR 700 billion for fleet expansion to boost revenue to IDR 2.2 trillion by 2025.

body: The Indonesian government's auction of sharia securities or government sukuk on Monday received IDR 13.85 trillion in bids, with a total of IDR 9 trillion won from seven series offered. Since the beginning of the year, the government has held 23 state sukuk auctions, accumulating a total of IDR 206.82 trillion in nominal value.
title: Indonesian Government Raises IDR 9 Trillion from Sharia Securities Auction

body: PT Indika Energy Tbk (INDY) saw a 22.4% year-on-year revenue drop in 9M 2024 due to divestment and declining coal prices, but is investing in non-coal sectors, including a USD 429 million investment in the Awakmas gold mine, which has potential to generate over USD 3 billion revenue over its operational lifespan. The company is also expanding into logistics and energy infrastructure through its subsidiary Tripatra, and has acquired Natura Aromatik, a producer of essential oils, for USD 13.6 million, expecting significant revenue contributions from these ventures.
title: Indika Energy's revenue drops 22.4% due to coal price decline, but invests in non-coal sectors including gold mining and logistics.

body: PT Avia Avian TBK (AVIA) received an interim cash dividend income of IDR 99.99 billion from its subsidiary PT Tirtakencana Tatawarna, representing 99.99% of the company's share ownership. As of September 30, 2024, Tirta's total assets reached IDR 4.06 trillion, while Avia recorded revenue of IDR 5.4 trillion and net profit of IDR 1.16 trillion in the first nine months of 2024.
title: Avia Avian receives IDR 99.99 billion interim cash dividend from subsidiary Tirtakencana Tatawarna.

body: PT Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADRO) has allocated a final cash dividend of $2.62 billion from its 2023 financial year, to be paid to shareholders, following a decision made at an extraordinary general meeting. The company reported a net profit of $1.64 billion, with a retained earnings balance of $5.15 billion and total equity of $7.40 billion.
title: Adaro Energy Allocates $2.62 Billion in Final Cash Dividend to Shareholders from 2023 Financial Year.

body: PT Tripar Multivision Plus Tbk (MVP) has expanded its Platinum Cineplex theatre network to Sragen, Central Java, marking its 14th location, as part of its strategy to tap into tier-2 and tier-3 cities in Indonesia with promising prospects and inadequate theatre infrastructure. The company, through its subsidiary PT Platinum Sinema, aims to provide a world-class theatre experience to people in these underserved areas, further solidifying its position in the Indonesian cinema market.
title: PT Tripar Multivision Plus Opens Platinum Cineplex Theatre in Sragen, Central Java

body: Akulaku Group, a major shareholder of PT Bank Neo Commerce Tbk (BNC), plans to divest 30% of its shares in BNC over 5 years, with a 2% annual reduction, following approval from 94.06% of shareholders. The divestment plan may involve other corporate actions, including a rights issue, and will bring Akulaku Group's stake in BNC to a maximum of 30%.
title: Akulaku Group to Gradually Divest 30% Stake in PT Bank Neo Commerce Over 5 Years

body: PT MD Entertainment Tbk (FILM) has provided a guarantee for deposit loan facilities worth IDR 28.5 billion to its subsidiaries, including PT MDTV Media Technologies Tbk, PT Net Media Digital, and others, with PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) as the creditor. The company claims the guarantee will not impact its financial condition or business continuity, and is optimistic about driving business expansion through its recent acquisition of 60.98% of NetV, gaining additional access to television broadcast.
title: PT MD Entertainment provides guarantee for loan facilities to its subsidiaries worth IDR 28.5 billion.

body: PT TBS Energi Utama TBK's shareholders approved a plan to restructure its subsidiaries, including PT Toba Bara Energi, PT Gorontalo Listrik Perdana, and PT Minahasa Cahaya Lestari, which involves divesting shares and transferring payables. The subsidiaries hold total assets of USD 337.59 million, USD 288.27 million, and USD 274.94 million, respectively, and the plan was approved by 56.495% of independent shareholders, representing 84.474% of total valid votes.
title: PT TBS Energi Utama's shareholders approve plan to restructure subsidiaries through divestment and transfer of shares.

body: Sugiman Halim, an independent investor, acquired 6.09% of PT Newport Marine Services Tbk (NMMS) and 9.85% of PT Global Sukses Digital Tbk (GSD), while Cathay Utima Investment Pte Ltd increased its stake in PT Suparma Tbk (SPMA) to nearly 40%. Meanwhile, other investors made notable transactions, including PT Samel Tumbuh Bersama selling 53.01 million shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS) and Cascade Gold Limited releasing 750,000 shares of PT Pabrik Kertas Tjiwi Kimia Tbk (TKIM).
title: Investors Make Significant Moves in Indonesian Stock Market with Notable Purchases and Sales of Shares in Various Companies.

body: PT Pinago Utama Tbk (PNGO) signed an investment credit facility agreement with PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) worth IDR 170 billion, consisting of two tranches with tenors of 60 months and 12 months, to boost its cash flow and business sustainability. Additionally, PNGO and BMRI extended their working capital credit agreement and treasury line facility until December 14, 2025, and PNGO also obtained an avalist line facility from BMRI for IDR 13.89 billion to finance palm plasma koperasi produsen sejahtera mandiri.
title: PT Pinago Utama Tbk secures IDR 170 billion investment credit facility from PT Bank Mandiri Tbk to boost cash flow and business sustainability.

body: Foreign investors bought a net 571.83 million shares, with top purchases including PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 550.58 million shares, PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 230.61 million shares, and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk with 41.96 million shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold a significant number of shares in PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with -110.77 million shares, PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) with -29.20 million shares, and PT Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADRO) with -70.98 million shares.
title: Foreign investors buy 571.83 million shares in Indonesian market, led by digital company GoTo and coal mining business Bumi Resources.

body: PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk (ATLA) debuted on the Indonesia Stock Exchange in April 2024, reporting a net profit of IDR 3.79 billion, a 102% increase from IDR 1.87 billion in 9M 2023, driven by a 89% year-on-year revenue growth to IDR 58.25 billion. The company raised IDR 120 billion in fresh funds from its initial public offering, with total equity and assets standing at IDR 167.9 billion and IDR 180.14 billion, respectively, as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia's Net Profit Surges 102% to IDR 3.79 Billion in 9M 2024.

body: PT Bakrie Telecom Tbk (BTEL) reported a loss of IDR 54.49 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 26% year-over-year decrease from IDR 73.66 billion in the same period last year, driven by a 129% increase in operating income to IDR 42.98 billion. The company's revenue was primarily contributed by its media infrastructure services, which generated IDR 83.69 billion, while its telecommunications services, digital advertising services, and information technology services generated IDR 4.11 billion, IDR 7.34 billion, and IDR 3.38 billion, respectively.
title: PT Bakrie Telecom reports lower loss of IDR 54.49 billion in first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Prima Andalan Prima Tbk (MCOL)'s interim dividend for the 2024 financial year decreased by 56% to IDR 350 or IDR 1.24 trillion, compared to IDR 888.89 billion in 2022 and IDR 250 per share in 2023. The dividend distribution is based on financial data as of September 2023, which showed a profit of USD 99.55 million, unrestricted retained earnings of USD 426.22 million, and total equity of USD 592 million.
title: PT Prima Andalan Prima Tbk's 2024 Interim Dividend Drops 56% from 2023.

body: PT Kawasan Industri Jababeka Tbk (KIJA) has revised its marketing sales target to IDR 3 trillion for 2024, driven by new government policy support for the property sector, and has already achieved IDR 2.39 trillion in marketing sales as of Q3 2024, a 38% increase from the same period last year. The company reported revenue of IDR 3.36 trillion in September 2024, up 47% year-on-year, with profit skyrocketing 232% to IDR 769.7 billion.
title: PT Kawasan Industri Jababeka targets IDR 3 trillion in marketing sales this year, driven by government policy support for the property sector.

body: PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk (MBMA) has secured a USD 200 million financing facility from PT Merdeka Tsingshan Indonesia (MTI) to meet its corporate general needs, with the loan value not exceeding 20% of its total equity of USD 2.32 billion as of June 30, 2024. As of June 30, 2024, MBMA had cash and cash equivalents of USD 247.16 million, and management believes the facility is more efficient than third-party loans due to MBMA's 80% control of MTI through PT Batutua Pelita Investama.
title: Merdeka Battery Materials secures USD 200 million financing facility from Tsingshan Indonesia for corporate general needs.

body: PT Adhi Karya TBK (ADHI) reported a 194.51% year-over-year profit surge to IDR 69.32 billion in Q3 2024, driven by efficiency in operating expenses and income tax expenses, despite a 19.97% revenue decline to IDR 9.16 trillion. The company's engineering construction segment contributed IDR 7.20 trillion in revenue, followed by manufacturing, property, shipping, and investment-concession segments, with key clients including PT Jasamarga Jogka Solo, PT Jasamarga Jogja Bawean, and the Ministry of Public Works and Housing.
title: PT Adhi Karya's Profit Soars 194.51% in Q3 2024 Despite 19.97% Revenue Decline

body: Robby Adijaya acquired a 5.11% stake in PT Ginting Jaya Energi Tbk (GJAE) by purchasing 126.56 million shares, while other investors made notable transactions, including PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk (PANS) increasing its share portion by 6% and PT Lautan Luas Tbk (LTLS) buying back 1.53 million of its own shares. Meanwhile, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama released 64 million shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS), and Ruhong Holding Pte Ltd maintained its 87.71% stake in PT Perma Plasindo Tbk (PPIS).
title: Robby Adijaya and other investors make notable transactions in Indonesian stocks, including purchases and sales of shares in various companies.

body: PT Agung Podomoro Land Tbk (APLN) has divested its assets in Ciawi, West Java, including the Hotel Pullman Ciawi Vimala Hills Resort Spa & Convention, to boost its cash flow and lower debt expenses, with its cash and cash equivalents increasing to IDR 1.06 trillion as of September 30, 2024. The company's total assets stood at IDR 27.14 trillion, with short-term liabilities at IDR 4.63 trillion and long-term liabilities at IDR 9.2 trillion, totaling IDR 13.91 trillion.
title: PT Agung Podomoro Land's subsidiary divests assets in Ciawi to boost cash flow and reduce debt.

body: PT Gerbangmas Tunggal Sejahtera acquired 44.65 billion shares of PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) through PT Sinarmas Sekuritas, gaining direct control over approximately 9.37% of shares, while PT Pangan Sejahtera Investama acquired 1 million shares of PT FKS Food Sejahtera Tbk, accounting for 58.26% of total shares. Meanwhile, several companies, including PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk, PT Nippon Indosari Corpindo Tbk, and PT Lautan Luas Tbk, conducted share buyback activities, and various investors sold shares of PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk, PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk, PT ITSec Asia Tbk, and PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in ownership and share buyback activities.

body: President Director of PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), Jahja Setiaatmadja, believes that the ongoing Ukraine-Russia war will impact the US's ability to achieve its 2.2% GDP growth target in 2025, and notes that the US Federal Reserve's 75-basis-point interest rate cut has instead led to a 70-bps increase in long-term treasury bills' yield. Setiaatmadja also highlights the challenges facing Indonesia's banking industry, citing the attractive yields of government securities and retail bonds, which make it difficult to lower deposit interest rates.
title: President Director of Bank Central Asia predicts Trump's victory will impact Ukraine-Russia conflict and US economy.
